forecast.ground_truth: in-repo was right — cabt needs corpus-engine infra that exists in-repo and was absent on main (a verifiable structural fact); the worktree was over-engineering. The operator reframed the call; the infra reality settled it.
forecast.label: build cabt in-repo, not in a git worktree
forecast.proposition: building in-repo (not a worktree) is the right call for cabt

forecast.ground_truth: live on the ladder, v2 = 585.7 — below μ₀=600 but a valid, legal, never-crash baseline.
forecast.label: ship the determinized PIMC floor as the baseline agent
forecast.proposition: a determinized PIMC engine over the real cg.api is a valid, live baseline

forecast.ground_truth: killed locally with a 0.40 margin (a large effect); never reached the ladder.
forecast.label: reject the card-aware (HP/KO-threat) value function
forecast.proposition: the local eval correctly rejects card-aware augury (it does not improve self-play)

forecast.ground_truth: ladder v3 = 539.4 < v2(n4) = 585.7 — BUT §5b proved n4≈n16 is below the resolution of any feasible-N (N≈30) self-play eval; the apparent miss is noise on both ends. The difference was UNRESOLVABLE, not resolved-against.
forecast.label: ship n_worlds=16 (more determinizations) over n_worlds=4
forecast.proposition: n_worlds=16 beats n_worlds=4 on the ladder by a margin the available games can resolve

forecast.ground_truth: both stronger decks LOST to the sample deck locally → the deck⊗engine insight: a setup deck needs a strong pilot, and ours was the bottleneck. Untested on the ladder (rejected before submission), so the resolution is structural, not ladder-measured.
forecast.label: reject the Mega Lucario ex + mono-Fighting aggro decks
forecast.proposition: the stronger decks do not beat the sample deck in self-play for our weak pilot (correctly rejected)

forecast.ground_truth: killed; v1 was authored blind from an enum list and attacked before developing. Re-authored as v2 AFTER grounding on the real obs — the FunSearch loop's diagnose step.
forecast.label: reject the blind-authored FunSearch heuristic v1
forecast.proposition: the local eval correctly rejects the blind-authored heuristic v1 (it loses in self-play)

forecast.ground_truth: ladder v4 climbed to 648.1 — above μ₀=600 and clearly > v2(585.7)/v3(539.4). The only agent above baseline. The large local effect TRANSFERRED (confirming the §5b rule: large effects are real and resolve on the ladder).
forecast.label: ship the grounded FunSearch v2 heuristic as the default agent
forecast.proposition: the grounded develop-first heuristic beats our PIMC search engine on the real ladder

forecast.ground_truth: FALSIFIED for our pilot — both stronger decks lost to the sample deck (decision reject-meta-aggro-decks). The deck lever does NOT transfer to a weak pilot; the PILOT was the bottleneck. An imported belief, not re-grounded to our context.
forecast.label: import the field's belief that 'deck is the biggest lever'
forecast.local_evidence: field dig: deck swing 423→893; assumed transferable
forecast.proposition: the field's 'deck is the biggest lever' (423→893) transfers to our agent
